name: Blockbuilders Design Concept
description: Design concept derived directly from docs/design/design-system.pen. The Pen file is the golden source and this document describes the visual system represented by its two top-level boards.

fileCharacteristics.nature: Static specimen board documenting tokens, typography, and component exemplars

sourceOfTruth.syncRule: If this JSON conflicts with the Pen file, the Pen file wins.

brandIdentity.name: Blockbuilders
brandIdentity.tagline: Visual Strategy Lab for Crypto Traders
brandIdentity.targetAudience: Retail crypto traders who want a professional, institution-inspired strategy-building interface without coding.
brandIdentity.positioning: Blockbuilders should feel closer to a disciplined trading workstation than to a playful no-code canvas.

corePrinciples.name: Signal Over Decoration
corePrinciples.description: The Pen boards use flat fills, thin rules, restrained shadows, and no decorative gradients. Information hierarchy comes from scale, spacing, and semantic color instead of ornament.

corePrinciples.name: Institutional Readability
corePrinciples.description: IBM Plex Sans and IBM Plex Mono create a more technical, terminal-adjacent tone than the previous system font direction. Numeric data, labels, and metadata should feel crisp and deliberate.

corePrinciples.name: Dense But Ordered
corePrinciples.description: The board carries a lot of information, but every section is separated by generous 64px spacing and repeated divider patterns. The interface should support data density without visual noise.

corePrinciples.name: Color Means Category
corePrinciples.description: Blue is the main action and information color. Green, red, and amber are reserved for outcome semantics. Purple appears in canvas input-node accents, not as the primary brand color.

corePrinciples.name: Stable Light/Dark Parity
corePrinciples.description: The dark board is not a stylistic remix of the light board. It is a direct counterpart with equivalent spacing, hierarchy, and component structure, using darker surfaces and brighter semantic accents.

designPhilosophy.designMood: Institutional trading interface with editorial restraint. Clean, exact, and engineered rather than glossy or trend-driven.

visualIdentity.description: Variant E is defined by flat surfaces, thin dividers, compact component geometry, IBM Plex typography, and semantic color used with restraint.
techniques: Large quiet canvas around content blocks instead of decorative backgrounds
No gradient headings, glassmorphism, or blurred frosted surfaces in the golden source
1px divider lines between sections and within compound components
4px base corner radius for nearly all surfaces, with 3px checkboxes and 6px tabs only where called for
Two-layer subtle surface shadow for cards and specimen blocks
IBM Plex Mono for metadata, dates, and performance numbers
Blue accent used for primary action, active state, and informational feedback

colorStrategy.philosophy: The golden source defines a blue-led signal system, not a purple-led brand. Blue carries the primary brand/action role, while purple is limited to the input node category on the canvas.

primary.usage: Primary buttons, active selections, informational accents, input focus/select states

supportingAccent.usage: Tertiary accent swatches and cool supporting surfaces

observations: The Pen file does not use ambient radial gradients.
The Pen file does not use transparent glass layers or backdrop blur.
Surface contrast is built with solid fills and thin borders.

darkModeStrategy.approach: Direct thematic inversion with stronger semantic brightness, deeper base surfaces, and equivalent geometry.
principles: Swap white and near-black foundations for #0C0C0E and #EAEAEC
Replace the light border #EEF0F2 with dark divider #2A2A2E
Promote semantic accents slightly brighter in dark mode
Keep spacing, typography, and component proportions identical between themes

typographyStrategy.philosophy: IBM Plex Sans handles interface copy and headings; IBM Plex Mono handles data, timestamps, and compact metadata. This pairing is a core part of the Signal identity.

eyebrow.usage: Variant labels and small section headings

sectionTitle.usage: Card titles and major section headers

bodyLarge.usage: Board subtitle and large supporting copy

uiBody.usage: Default UI text, inputs, breadcrumbs, descriptions

label.usage: Buttons, labels, tabs, alerts, node titles

captionMono.usage: Token labels, specimen annotations, parameter metadata

principles: Use IBM Plex Mono whenever the value is numeric, date-based, machine-like, or comparative
Use letter spacing only on high-level headings and specimen labels where the Pen file explicitly shows it
Do not reintroduce gradient text styles; they are absent from the golden source
Keep text colors binary and purposeful: primary foreground for key content, muted text for context

spacingStrategy.philosophy: Spacing is regular, modular, and deliberately repetitive. The board relies on predictable 4px-family increments with a few larger framing values.

layoutStrategy.philosophy: The Pen file is a wide desktop specimen board documenting a single design direction in light and dark themes. It is not a responsive layout blueprint.

boardStructure.flow: Single vertical stack of labeled sections separated by divider rules

patterns.titleBlock: Eyebrow label, large title, then a fixed-width descriptive paragraph
patterns.tokenRows: Two five-column swatch rows for the core palette
patterns.typographyGrid: Four vertically stacked rows, each pairing mono annotation at left with live text specimen at right
patterns.buttonRow: Five side-by-side action variants on one line
patterns.formRow: Three side-by-side labeled fields for input/select examples
patterns.canvasNodeRow: Five node specimens across a single row, each using the same structural shell
patterns.metricCardRow: Four evenly sized metric cards with label, icon, value, and supporting line
patterns.sidebarSpecimen: Single 260px sidebar example with header, nav stack, and footer
patterns.stateRow: Three equal-height panels for loading, empty, and error states
patterns.headerAndBreadcrumb: Standalone thin-strip exemplars for app chrome

limitationsOfTheGoldenSource: No breakpoint behavior is documented in the Pen file
No mobile-specific layouts are encoded in the Pen file
No animation timings or motion choreography are encoded in the Pen file

surfaces: Tabs container uses 6px outer radius with 4px inner active tab radius
Primary surface separation is solid fill plus 1px border
The same two-layer shadow recipe is reused for cards, nodes, and empty/error/loading state panels
interactionHierarchy: Secondary emphasis in dark mode can be inverted light fill on dark surface
Outline actions use only a 1px border and foreground text
Ghost/cancel actions remove border and background entirely
Destructive actions use red text with faint tinted background and border
stateTreatment: Success, warning, error, and info states combine colored icon, colored text, and low-opacity tinted background
Loading, empty, and error are represented as centered blocks with restrained supporting copy
Status badges pair semantic tint backgrounds with a colored dot and medium-weight label
canvasLanguage: All node categories share identical structure and geometry
Category meaning is expressed through icon/tag tint only, not through different shapes
Node metadata is compact and mono-styled

principles: Favor 14px icons inside compact controls and 16px icons in alerts and metric cards
Use 20px icons for chrome actions such as menu and notifications
Use 24px icons only for large empty/error state symbols

accessibilityCommitments: Semantic status is always paired with readable text, not color alone
Metrics and metadata use mono typography for scanability and alignment
Borders remain visible in both themes through explicit 1px contrast rules
Alert and state specimens keep copy concise and actionable instead of purely decorative

tradingAndBacktesting: Performance metrics must feel exact and machine-readable
Positive and negative outcomes should be instantly distinguishable through green and red
Data-dense UI should remain calm by using muted neutrals around the highlighted signals
canvasEditing: Node taxonomy is category-first and color-assisted
Node shells remain visually consistent so traders learn one mental model
Parameter text belongs in the supporting mono/body layer, not the display layer

usePrimaryBlueWhen: The user is taking the main action on the page
A tab, selection, or navigation item is active
Informational status needs emphasis without implying warning or danger
useNeutralSurfacesWhen: Creating chrome such as cards, headers, sidebars, and tabs
You want hierarchy through spacing and typography instead of decoration
useMonoTypographyWhen: Showing dates, symbols, timeframes, metrics, counts, or machine-like metadata
Labelling token values or board annotations
avoid: Purple-led branding in primary UI actions
Soft oversized radii that dilute the technical character
Invented mobile behavior not represented by the Pen source
